article_title: Life Biosciences Raises $80 Million to Fund First Human Trial of Epigenetic Cell Rejuvenation

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: Life Biosciences Raises $80 Million to Fund First Human Trial of Epigenetic Cell Rejuvenation
content_preview.summary: The Boston biotech closes a Series D round to advance ER-100, the first FDA-cleared therapy that uses partial epigenetic reprogramming to reverse cellular aging in humans.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Life Biosciences, a Boston-based clinical-stage biotechnology company, announced on April 8 the close of a fully subscribed $80 million Series D financing round. The proceeds will fund the completion of its Phase 1 clinical trial of ER-100, a gene therapy that uses partial epigenetic reprogramming to restore aged and damaged cells to a younger functional state, according to the company's [press release](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/08/3270043/0/en/Life-Bioscien...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent biotech article with strong structure. Clear explanation of the PER platform mechanism (Yamanaka factors) accessible to general readers. Well-balanced What We Don't Know section.
editor_notes.source_verification: 2 sources checked. GlobeNewsWire press release: VERIFIED — confirms $80M Series D, fully subscribed, ER-100, Phase 1 trial NCT07290244, Q1 2026 initiation, IND clearance Jan 28 2026, glaucoma/NAION indications, OCT4/SOX2/KLF4 factors, Jerry McLaughlin CEO, runway to H2 2027. Fortune: VERIFIED — confirms David Sinclair co-founder, FDA IND clearance, non-human primate NAION reversal studies, three-factor approach, Altos Labs $3B. Note: Fortune mentions NewLimit at $130M Series B, while article claims $280M total — may reflect cumulative funding. Retro Biosciences $1.2B figure not in Fortune extract but widely reported elsewhere. Human-requested article reviewed with heightened scrutiny.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core Life Biosciences claims all verified: $80M Series D, ER-100, Phase 1 trial details, IND clearance date, Sinclair co-founder, McLaughlin CEO. Minor note: competitive landscape figures (NewLimit $280M, Retro $1.2B) sourced to Fortune but may reflect broader public knowledge beyond the specific article text. These are context, not core claims.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and scientific throughout. Appropriately cautious about translational prospects. No hype despite the sensational-sounding subject matter.
editor_notes.originality: No prior longevity/epigenetic reprogramming coverage. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong human-requested biotech article. Core claims verified against primary sources. Competitive landscape figures are widely reported public knowledge. Approved.
